channel.title: Home on Adrian's Website
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Adrian's Website

item.title: Symbolic Regression for a Non-elementary Integral
item.description: Closed-form approximations can be discovered for integrals without solutions in terms of elementary functions through symbolic regression

item.title: Autonomous Robotic Grasping for On-orbit Satellite Repair
item.description: Path planning and controller design in MATLAB of a robotic arm that for grasping spinning satellites in orbit

item.title: Inverted Pendulum on a Cart
item.description: Design of a feedback loop for the stabilization of an inverted pendulum on a cart

item.title: Model Aircraft CFD Analysis
item.description: Stability and performance analysis of an aircraft using computational fluid dynamics (CFD)

item.title: Prototype Realization
item.description: Design, build, and fly a humanitarian Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV)

item.title: About Me
item.description: During middle school and high school, I used to participate in various mathematics and physics competitions organized at the local level (school- or city-wise). I also used to attend some extracurricular lessons in physics held rotatively by teachers from different schools in my hometown. Whenever a contest’s profile was more creative, I scored well and received small awards and even qualified for the contest’s national phase. These activities instilled competitiveness in me from a young age. In college I have continued participating in the student contests for strength of materials, mechanics, and mathematics with national-level results. It turned out that all these years were excellent training for what was about to be the biggest challenge of my professional life: completing a PhD.

item.title: Publications
item.description:
Journal Articles

  • S. M. T. Zaidi, A. Arustei, A. Munir and A. Dutta, Single-Agent Attention Actor–Critic: A Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Solution for Low-Thrust Spacecraft Trajectory Optimization, in IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems, vol. 61, no. 5, pp. 11628-11643, Oct. 2025, doi: 10.1109/TAES.2025.3569615
  • S. M. T. Zaidi, A. Arustei, A. Munir and A. Dutta, Automated Trajectory Planning: A Cascaded Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach for Low-Thrust Spacecraft Orbit-Raising, IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Magazine, Jan 2025. doi: 10.1109/MAES.2025.3556795.
  • A. Arustei, A. Dutta, Direct Optimization of Low-thrust Orbit-raising Maneuvers Using Adjoint Sensitivities, Acta Astronautica, Vol. 219, 2024, pp. 965–981, doi: 10.1016/j.actaastro.2024.03.059.

Conference Papers

  • J. Steck, A. Arustei, A. Dutta. Low Thrust Minimum Time Transfer: Reinforcement Learning of Optimal Co-State Time Dependance, AIAA 2026-2782, AIAA SCITECH 2026 Forum, January 2026. (in press)
  • A. Arustei, A. Dutta, Nested B-spline Control and Modified (h,e) Elements for Lengthy Low-thrust Cislunar Orbit-raising, in: AAS/AIAA Astrodynamics Specialist Conference, Boston, Massachusetts, USA, 2025, AAS 25-874.
  • J. Steck, A. Arustei, A. Dutta, Low Thrust Minimum Time Transfer: Single Network Adaptive Critic Approximate Dynamic Programming, 2025 AAS/AIAA Space Flight Mechanics Meeting, AAS 2025-311. (in press)
  • A. Dutta, A. Arustei, M. Chace, P. Chadalavada, J. Steck, T. Zaidi, A. Munir, Machine Learning Assisted Low-Thrust Orbit-Raising: A Comparative Assessment of a Sequential Algorithm and Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach, AIAA SCITECH 2024 Forum, Jan 2024, doi: 10.2514/6.2024-1669.
  • A. Arustei, A. Dutta, An adjoint sensitivity method for the sequential low-thrust orbit raising problem, in: AAS/AIAA Astrodynamics Specialist Conference, Charlotte, North Carolina, USA, 2022, AAS 22-835.

Theses/Dissertations

  • A. Arustei, Computational Graph Construction of Adjoint Sensitivities for Low-thrust Trajectory Optimization and Desensitized Optimal Control, Wichita State University, Doctoral Dissertation, 2026.
  • A. Arustei, Development of a System Identification Tool for Subscale Flight Testing, Linköping University, Master’s Thesis, 2019, DiVA id: diva2:1324418.
